headline: Trump's Gaza committee remains an empty shell paralyzed by Israel
description: Created four months ago as part of Donald Trump's peace plan, the entity is supposed to manage the coastal territory in place of Hamas. But Israel is preventing its members from entering, leaving them stranded in their offices in Cairo.

headline: EU's Séjourné warns Europe could splinter without a trade 'power struggle' with China
Norway to join France-led nuclear deterrence program
France to expand access to weight-loss drugs for obesity patients
description: The obesity treatments Wegovy and Mounjaro will be covered starting in mid-June in France, under a controlled framework. This measure will cost the state an estimated €100 million annually.

headline: Morocco faces over 40,000 job losses after France bans telemarketing calls
description: A law passed in France bans all prospecting calls without the customer's explicit consent starting August 11. The move endangers tens of thousands of employees in Moroccan call centers, which are heavily dependent on the French market.

headline: In Brittany, luxury tourism is taking over the coastline
description: In Saint-Malo, a court granted approval at the end of March for the construction of a 4- and 5-star hotel complex and a thalassotherapy center on the site of a former municipal campground. This luxury project is one of many springing up across the region.

headline: By backtracking on environmental progress, Macron is 'betraying' the French, former members of the Climate Convention write
description: The signatories of this open letter warn about the government's dismantling of the Climate and Resilience law and call on citizens to defend 'their' law.

description: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.
byline: Jean-Michel Décugis,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Nicolas-Charles Torrent

headline: From a kidnapping in France to crypto wallet in Venezuela: Inside a ransom laundering scheme
description: An investigation into the kidnapping of a cryptocurrency entrepreneur's father has uncovered an unexpected criminal network, highlighting the complexity of the financial channels criminals use to launder money.

headline: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
description: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.
